verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 encodes a type II single-pass TM glycoprotein. Its N-terminal hydrophobic segment functions as both signal anchor and TM domain, retaining the protein in the Golgi as the canonical residence for glycosyltransferase activity. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to also traffic to the plasma membrane, where it is present on the cell surface — particularly on sperm (zona pellucida binding), on epithelial cells, and on various tumor cells. On the PM outer leaflet, the catalytic domain faces extracellularly and has been detected by surface biotinylation and functional assays (it acts as a lectin/signaling molecule binding polylactosamine on growth factor receptors). This constitutes a documented minority PM pool alongside the dominant Golgi pool — a dual-localization pattern. The surface pool is best described as contextual because the Golgi is the dominant compartment and PM display varies by cell type and state.
